name: Q3148835
abstract: Cliff Leeman (September 10, 1913 – April 26, 1986) was an American jazz drummer. His nickname was "Mr.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Eddie Condon and Ben Webster

works.t: Baby, Won't You Please Come Home
I Gotta Right to Sing the Blues
Lady, be Good!: Oh, Lady, Be Good!

quotes.text: Well, there is no date on the album, but I think it was around '55. I was called for a record date at Carnegie Hall rehearsal hall, that's where it took place and they had set up equipment there, and when I walked in, I was flabbergasted the people on the date. In the first place, I walked in - and I always walk in early to set up the drums; there was Walter Pope there, and we said greetings and Joe Newman was there, and we had just begun to know each other, really; and Lawrence Brown on trombone; Pete Brown on alto sax -- now he'd been ill, and they found him. He hadn't played for a couple of years, and Frank Wes$ on tenor saxophone, and Pete Johnson on piano. Now the last time I saw or heard about Pete was at the Sunset Club, which we covered back in Kansas City in 1935, and all these people were in Kansas City in 1935. Freddie Greene was there on guitar, and
quotes.caption: Recalling a 1955 Carnegie Hall recording session

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Teagarden and Maxie and Peanuts Hucko and Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins
about_quotes.evidence: It was myself, no bass player, Cliff Leeman was the drummer, Cutty Cutshall, great trombone player, Peanuts Hucko on clarinet and either Buck Clayton or Yank Lawson on trumpet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: when we played in the trio, we played -- we did a duet date since we were recording a duet, we had Cliff Leeman on drums

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Cliff Leeman was there for a while... And Eddie Safranski was playing bass... Claude Williamson was the piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: I had such a good relation with him that -- I would take my vacation, I could put somebody in my place, and then, like I said, during the war, Woody Herman's band -- Woody came to me and this was with Dave Tough and Cliff Leeman... I worked in his place.

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: I think the longest time band was probably Edmond Hall and Cutty Cutshall, and Schroeder and Cliff Leeman... I got hold of Cliff Leeman and I got hold of the piano player, you know, Ralph Sutton and we lined the whole thing up
